document.title: AspenView Software Length Measurement Accuracy Validation Report

sourceSummary.oneLine: Validation report describing verified length and angle measurement accuracy for AspenView Digital X-ray Acquisition Software using pixel spacing values in X-ray images.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports verification of AspenView software measurement-tool accuracy by comparing software measurements with phantom object measurements and manual pixel-spacing calculations.
keyPoints: AspenView calculates actual length by multiplying number of pixels by pixel spacing value in mm/pixel.
Verification used AspenView Digital X-ray Acquisition Software with an Aspen Imaging Healthcare IODR 1717 X-ray detector and 140 micron pixel spacing.
A 150 mm section measured by pixel calculation produced 149.94 mm, an error of 0.06 mm or 0.04%.
Ten AspenView measurements of a 150 mm object averaged 150.201 mm, with average error rate 0.13%.
Ten AspenView measurements of a 90 degree object averaged 90.05 degrees, with average error rate 0.05%.
The report recommends monthly periodic verification and re-validation whenever AspenView software is updated.

evidence.claim: The document is an AspenView Software Length Measurement Accuracy Validation Report.
evidence.evidenceText: AspenView Software Length Measurement Accuracy Validation Report
evidence.pageTarget: AspenView Software Length Measurement Accuracy Validation Report

evidence.claim: The report applies to AspenView software minimum version 1.1.40.0 or higher.
The verification concerns AspenView Digital X-ray Acquisition Software length measurement accuracy.
evidence.evidenceText: This report is about description of verified length measurement accuracy of the AspenView Digital X-ray Acquisition software.

evidence.claim: The verification was performed with an X-ray detector from Aspen Imaging Healthcare.
evidence.evidenceText: The verification was performed on the AspenView software used with an X -ray detector from the manufacturer Aspen Imaging Healthcare

evidence.claim: The detector model used was IODR 1717.
evidence.evidenceText: X- ray detector : Manufacturer Aspen Imaging Healthcare (Model number: IODR 1717)

evidence.claim: The pixel spacing value used was 140 microns, or 0.140 mm.
evidence.pageTarget: Pixel spacing value in X-ray image measurement

evidence.claim: The phantom object was a Resolution Test Pattern, P/N 800437, by SUN NUCLEAR.
evidence.evidenceText: Phantom objects: Resolutoin Test Pattern (P/N: 800437 by SUN NUCLEAR)

evidence.claim: AspenView calculates actual length by multiplying pixel count by pixel spacing value.
evidence.evidenceText: Actual length ( mm ) = Number of pixels × Pixel Spacing Value ( mm / pixel )

evidence.claim: A 150 mm section measured as 1071 pixels calculated to 149.94 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Number of pixels measured: 1071 pixels; Calculated length: 1071 × 0.140 mm = 149.94 mm

evidence.claim: The 150 mm pixel-spacing calculation error was 0.06 mm, or 0.04%, within the 1% acceptable range.
evidence.evidenceText: This result shows an error of 0.06 mm at 150 mm in length, which is 0.04 %, within the acceptable range for error rate. (Error rate within 1%)

evidence.claim: Ten AspenView measurements of the same 150 mm object averaged 150.201 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: AspenView Software 150 mm Measurement average : 150.201 mm

evidence.claim: The 150 mm object measurement average error rate was 0.13%.
evidence.evidenceText: the error rate was 0.13%, which was within 1%. (Table 1)

evidence.claim: Ten AspenView measurements of the same 90 degree object averaged 90.05 degrees.
evidence.evidenceText: AspenView Software 90 degrees Measurement average : 90.05 degrees

evidence.claim: The 90 degree object measurement average error rate was 0.05%.
evidence.evidenceText: the error rate was 0.05%, which was within 1%. (Table 2)

evidence.claim: The report concludes AspenView measured length and angle values were close to actual object values and average error rates were within 1%.
evidence.evidenceText: We confirmed that the measured values using AspenView software are very close to the actual object length and angle. The average error rates are both within 1%, which is within the acceptable range for medical image analysis.

evidence.claim: The report recommends repeating verification at least once a month.
evidence.evidenceText: Periodic Verification: We recommend that you repeat this verification process at least once a month to ensure the ongoing accuracy of the system.

evidence.claim: The report requires re-validation whenever AspenView software is updated.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Updates: Re-validation is required whenever AspenView software is updated.
